project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

E.name: Proactive Intelligence
E.notes: Sweep pipeline working end-to-end (130 facts stored). Sonnet extraction fixed. Context planner built but untested.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Training wheels removed. Pre-tool security checks + output sanitization remain.

G.name: GitHub + Self-Development
G.notes: Redesigned as self-development system. Three-gate safety model (branch isolation, pre-deploy verification, post-deploy health check + auto-rollback). Design at docs/DESIGN_TRACK_G_SELF_DEV.md. Phase 1 (read-only GitHub) is safe to implement immediately.

H.name: Plugin Adapter + CC Bridge
H.notes: clawvato-memory plugin built as standalone repo (github.com/andrewcovato/clawvato-memory). Self-contained MCP server + SKILL.md. Session-scoped working context. Configured locally, not yet tested.

J.name: Hybrid Architecture
J.notes: Replaced by CC-Native Engine (Track O). Hybrid code preserved on main branch as fallback.

M.name: Background Sweeps
M.notes: Pipeline working end-to-end. 130 facts + 693 entities stored from first backfill. Sonnet extraction (not Haiku — 8K output limit).

N.name: Context Planner
N.notes: Superseded by CC-Native Engine — CC plans its own context natively.

O.name: CC-Native Engine
O.notes: Claude Code as core engine via Channels. Deployed on Railway (cc-native-engine branch). Replaces hybrid architecture. Design at docs/DESIGN_CC_NATIVE.md.

infrastructure.local: macOS, DATABASE_URL=... npx tsx src/cli/index.ts start
infrastructure.dataVolume: /data (Claude CLI auth + gws config + debug workspaces)
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — CC-native engine costs $0

currentSprint.name: CC-Native Engine + Shared Memory Brain
goals: CC-Native Engine via Channels ✅ (deployed on Railway, working)
Slack Channel MCP server ✅ (Socket Mode, debounce, reply/react/history tools)
Supervisor entrypoint with expect for trust prompt ✅
Idle timeout with verified handoff protocol ✅ (in system prompt)
clawvato-memory plugin repo ✅ (standalone MCP server, SKILL.md, session-scoped working context)
Local CC MCP config ✅ (wired to Railway Postgres)
Track G redesigned as self-development ✅ (design doc complete)
Swap Railway to use plugin MCP server ⬜
currentSprint.progress: ~85% complete. CC-native engine deployed and working. Memory plugin built but untested.

lastSession.type: Architecture pivot — CC-native engine + shared memory brain plugin
keyDecisions: CC-native engine: Claude Code as core via Channels, replacing custom hybrid orchestration (~2,250 lines)
On Max plan, Opus is $0 — cost-based routing unnecessary
Channels research preview: --dangerously-load-development-channels for custom channel servers
expect for PTY + trust prompt automation (ANSI codes break literal matching, use regex)
Trust prompt: hasTrustDialogAccepted in .claude.json does NOT prevent it — must use expect
Memory plugin as separate repo: any CC instance shares the same Postgres brain
Session-scoped working context: wctx:SESSION_ID:key pattern for multi-instance safety
Fact extraction: trust CC's Opus judgment + SessionEnd hook as safety net (not Slack-specific extraction)
Store what+why, never how — the noise filter for memory
Track G redesigned: self-development with three-gate safety model
Verified handoff protocol: subagent tests the handoff before session exit
Build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships, don't lock to today's constraints
GitHub auto-deploy from branch (not railway up CLI — was timing out due to .claude/ 379MB)

immediateBacklog.title: Test clawvato-memory plugin locally — restart CC session, verify search/store against Railway Postgres
Swap Railway's memory MCP to use the plugin server (with session-scoped working context)
Run cc-native engine for 24h, monitor for crashes, verify session restart + handoff

backlog.title: Track G Phase 1: read-only GitHub access (gh CLI in Docker, CC can read issues/PRs)
Track G Phase 2: branch + PR creation (self-development, owner-gated)
Wire sweep system to cc-native (task scheduler posts to Slack, CC handles sweeps)
Test verified handoff protocol — trigger idle timeout, verify subagent verification works
SessionEnd hook for memory extraction safety net
Artifact short-term memory (FIFO cache for follow-ups)
Track G Phase 3: self-deploy with health check + auto-rollback
Obsidian integration — .md export of memory for visibility

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE CODE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Always build for the future — adopt Anthropic tech as it ships, don't lock to today's constraints
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
Plan before coding — don't jump straight to implementation
Don't build bespoke tools/tables for specific entity types — unified memory handles it
Don't build point solutions — trust general-purpose tools (search_memory, etc.)
Store what+why, never how — implementation details don't belong in memory
MCP stdio servers MUST redirect ALL logging to stderr
CC trust prompt: hasTrustDialogAccepted in .claude.json does NOT prevent it — must use expect with regex
ANSI codes in CC TUI output break literal expect matching — use -re regex
--dangerously-skip-permissions does NOT skip trust prompt — separate security layer
--dangerously-skip-permissions cannot run as root — need non-root Docker user
su -p preserves HOME=/root — must explicitly export HOME=/home/clawvato
railway up uses .gitignore for upload filtering — .claude/ must be in .gitignore (379MB)
GitHub auto-deploy is more reliable than railway up CLI for deploys
Haiku max output 8192 tokens — use Sonnet for extraction
Zod schema defaults override config/default.json — must update BOTH
Google Drive classifies .ts files as text/texmacs — can't rely on MIME type alone
